project: Local LLM Server + OpenClaw + Copilot Coordination

tasks.title: Install Node.js LTS + npm
tasks.description: Install Node.js LTS and npm on the server. Needed for OpenClaw.
tasks.result: Node.js v24.14.0 + npm 11.9.0 installed.

tasks.title: Install GitHub CLI + auth
tasks.description: Install gh CLI and authenticate. Needed for repo management. (Skippable — PAT-based git push already working.)
tasks.result: Not needed — using fine-grained PAT for git push.

tasks.title: Create collab git repo
tasks.description: Create ~/collab/ dir, init git, create private GitHub repo PeerDiego/llm-collab, push.
tasks.result: Repo live at github.com/PeerDiego/llm-collab. PAT-based auth configured.

tasks.title: Cron: auto-sync collab repo
tasks.description: Cron job every 5 min — only commits+pushes if there are changes.
tasks.result: auto-sync.sh running via cron every 5 min. Dirty-check via git status --porcelain.

tasks.title: Install Ollama
tasks.description: Install Ollama for CPU-only LLM inference on this server.
tasks.result: Ollama installed, systemd enabled, CPU-only mode.

tasks.title: Pull LLM model
tasks.description: Pull a suitable quantized model (e.g., llama3.1:8b or qwen2.5-coder:7b) via Ollama.
tasks.result: llama3.1:8b pulled successfully.

tasks.title: Verify Ollama works
tasks.description: Test that Ollama is serving and responding to inference requests.
tasks.result: Ollama responding at localhost:11434. Tested inference OK.

tasks.title: Ollama systemd service
tasks.description: Ensure Ollama starts on boot via systemd.
tasks.result: Ollama systemd service enabled and running on boot.

tasks.title: Build MCP coordination server
tasks.description: Python MCP server with tools: get_plan, claim_task, complete_task, add_task, post_message, get_messages. Stores state in ~/collab/plan.json and messages.json.
tasks.result: MCP coordination server built (server.py). Tools: get_plan, get_my_tasks, claim_task, complete_task, add_task, block_task, post_message, get_messages.

tasks.title: Configure MCP for LLMCo
tasks.description: Add MCP server config to LLMCo's Copilot CLI so coordination tools are available.
tasks.result: MCP config added to ~/.copilot/mcp.json. Will take effect on next Copilot CLI restart.

tasks.title: Test MCP tools
tasks.description: Verify MCP tools work from LLMCo's Copilot CLI.
tasks.result: MCP server responds correctly to initialize + reads plan.json.

tasks.title: Install OpenClaw on server
tasks.description: Install OpenClaw via npm on this server.
tasks.result: OpenClaw 2026.3.13 installed globally via npm.

tasks.title: Configure OpenClaw for Ollama
tasks.description: Configure OpenClaw to use local Ollama at localhost:11434.
tasks.result: OpenClaw configured with ollama/llama3.1:8b as primary model. Config at ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json, env at ~/.openclaw/.env.

tasks.title: Write laptop setup instructions
tasks.description: Comprehensive instructions for LapCo: SSH tunnels, OpenClaw install+config, MCP connection, verification steps. Save to ~/collab/laptop-setup.md.
tasks.result: laptop-setup.md written with Linux/macOS + Windows instructions, MCP setup, and verification steps.

tasks.title: Configure SSH tunnel (Linux)
tasks.description: Set up an SSH tunnel from the Linux client to the LLM server to forward Ollama (port 11434). Run: ssh -N -L 11434:localhost:11434 <user>@<server> (add -f to background). Verify with: curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags
tasks.result: SSH tunnel established: ssh -p 45554 -f -N -L 11434:localhost:11434 claw@mw-016.myworkspace.microsoft.com. Verified: curl localhost:11434/api/tags shows phi3:medium, qwen2.5-coder:7b, llama3.1:8b.

tasks.title: Install + configure OpenClaw (Linux)
tasks.description: Install OpenClaw on the Linux client: npm install -g openclaw@latest. Create ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with primary model ollama/llama3.1:8b. Create ~/.openclaw/.env with OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama-local. Verify: openclaw config validate && openclaw models status.
tasks.result: OpenClaw 2026.3.13 installed globally via npm (Node 22.22.1). Config at ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with ollama/llama3.1:8b as primary model. Auth detected from env.

tasks.title: Set up MCP coordination server (Linux)
tasks.description: Install Python venv + mcp package: cd ~/collab && python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate && pip install mcp. Configure ~/.copilot/mcp.json with command pointing to ~/collab/.venv/bin/python3 and args [~/collab/server.py]. Restart Copilot CLI to activate. Verify with get_plan() tool.
tasks.result: Python venv created at ~/collab/.venv, mcp package installed, ~/.copilot/mcp.json configured with collab-coordinator pointing to server.py. MCP server tested and responding.

tasks.title: Verify full setup (Linux)
tasks.description: Verify the complete setup: 1) SSH tunnel active (curl localhost:11434/api/tags). 2) OpenClaw configured (openclaw models status shows ollama). 3) MCP tools available (get_plan returns task board). Mark done only when all three checks pass.
tasks.result: All 3 checks pass: 1) SSH tunnel active — 3 models visible (llama3.1:8b, qwen2.5-coder:7b, phi3:medium). 2) OpenClaw 2026.3.13 configured with ollama/llama3.1:8b. 3) MCP tools responding correctly.

tasks.title: Configure OpenClaw as persistent message-checking agent
tasks.description: Set up OpenClaw on the server as a persistent agent with two goals: (1) Heartbeat that periodically checks messages.json for unread messages addressed to claw-machine and responds, giving the server presence between Copilot CLI sessions. (2) Shared prompt proxy — act as a single warm OpenClaw instance that clients route through, maintaining one KV cache with the project system prompt pre-loaded. Clients send only user messages; the server-side agent handles the system prompt, eliminating redundant prompt eval across clients.
tasks.notes: Deferred for future implementation. OpenClaw and Ollama are already installed — this is a configuration task, not a build task.

tasks.title: Implement retract_message MCP tool
tasks.description: Add message retraction support to the MCP coordination server. Changes required: (1) Add auto-generated 8-char hex ID field to all messages in post_message. (2) New tool: retract_message(message_id, from_agent) — sets retracted=true, agents can only retract their own messages, commits+pushes via _sync_write. (3) Update get_messages to filter out retracted messages by default, with optional include_retracted=False param for audit/debug. Spec agreed with bright-spark via messages — see messages.json for full thread.
tasks.result: Implemented in server.py: (1) post_message auto-generates 8-char hex message IDs, (2) new retract_message(message_id, from_agent) tool with ownership check, (3) get_messages filters retracted by default with include_retracted param. Also added skip_pull param to get_messages earlier for efficient polling.

tasks.title: Add 10-minute task polling protocol to agent instructions
tasks.description: Add a task polling habit to AGENTS.md and each agent's copilot-instructions.md. Agents should check for newly assigned tasks every 10 minutes (using get_my_tasks or equivalent) and auto-claim pending tasks assigned to them. Similar pattern to the 3-minute message poll but less frequent. Waiting to validate the approach before updating docs.
tasks.result: Added 10-minute task polling protocol to AGENTS.md (pseudocode), ONBOARDING.md (step 7), and copilot-instructions.md (LLMCo). Broadcast sent to all agents to update their instructions and restart.

tasks.title: Re-benchmark OpenClaw with correct bootstrapTotalMaxChars setting
tasks.description: Re-run ollama-benchmark.py using the correct OpenClaw config knob. bootstrapMaxChars is per-file (default 20K), bootstrapTotalMaxChars is total prompt budget (default 150K). Test with reduced bootstrapTotalMaxChars values to find the sweet spot for CPU caching. Update openclaw-optimization.md with corrected findings. Post results to admin and bright-spark via message system when complete.
tasks.result: Re-benchmarked with correct knob (bootstrapTotalMaxChars). Caching cliff is ~40K chars (~4K tokens) on CPU. Recommendation updated to bootstrapTotalMaxChars=40000. Updated openclaw-optimization.md with both initial and re-benchmark results. Notified admin and bright-spark via messages.

tasks.title: Fix @all message read tracking (per-agent read_by)
tasks.description: Fix broadcast message read tracking: @all messages use read_by (list of agent IDs) instead of single read boolean. Update server.py get_messages, poll-messages.sh, and dashboard rendering. Dashboard determines @all read status by comparing read_by against agent list from plan.json.
tasks.result: Implemented per-agent read tracking for @all broadcasts. Direct messages keep read:true/false. Broadcasts use read_by:[agent-ids]. Updated server.py (get_messages + helpers), poll-messages.sh, and dashboard (compares read_by against agent list from plan.json). Notified bright-spark.

tasks.title: Configure SSH tunnel (Windows)
tasks.description: Set up an SSH tunnel from the Windows client to the LLM server to forward Ollama (port 11434). On WSL/Linux: ssh -N -L 11434:localhost:11434 <user>@<server>. On native Windows PowerShell: Start-Process ssh -ArgumentList "-N","-L","11434:localhost:11434","<user>@<server>" -WindowStyle Hidden. Verify with: curl http://localhost:11434/api/tags
tasks.result: SSH tunnel established: ssh -N -L 11434:localhost:11434 -p 3478 claw@135.233.2.31. Verified: 4 models available (llama3.1:8b, llama3.2:3b, qwen2.5-coder:7b, phi3:medium).

tasks.title: Install + configure OpenClaw (Windows)
tasks.description: Install OpenClaw on the Windows client: npm install -g openclaw@latest. Create ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with primary model ollama/llama3.1:8b. Create ~/.openclaw/.env with OLLAMA_API_KEY=ollama-local. Verify: openclaw config validate && openclaw models status.
tasks.result: Node.js LTS installed via winget. OpenClaw 2026.3.13 installed globally. Config at ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with ollama/llama3.1:8b. Auth detected from env (OLLAMA_API_KEY).

tasks.title: Set up MCP coordination server (Windows)
tasks.description: Install Python venv + mcp package: cd ~/collab && python -m venv .venv && .venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1 && pip install mcp. Configure ~/.copilot/mcp-config.json with command pointing to ~/collab/.venv/Scripts/python.exe and args [~/collab/server.py]. Restart Copilot CLI to activate. Verify with get_plan() tool.
tasks.result: Python venv created at ~/collab/.venv, mcp package installed. mcp-config.json configured. MCP server tools confirmed available in Copilot CLI.

tasks.title: Verify full setup (Windows)
tasks.description: Verify the complete setup: 1) SSH tunnel active (curl localhost:11434/api/tags). 2) OpenClaw configured (openclaw models status shows ollama). 3) MCP tools available (get_plan returns task board). Mark done only when all three checks pass.
tasks.result: All 3 checks pass: 1) SSH tunnel active — 4 models (llama3.1:8b, llama3.2:3b, qwen2.5-coder:7b, phi3:medium). 2) OpenClaw 2026.3.13 configured with ollama/llama3.1:8b, auth from env. 3) MCP collab-coordinator tools available in Copilot CLI.

tasks.title: Tell the user a joke
tasks.description: desktop-comet's user has requested a surprise joke. Post it as a message to 'all' via the MCP post_message tool. Make it a good one!
tasks.result: Joke posted to all agents via broadcast message.

tasks.title: Benchmark with OpenClaw's actual prompt sizes
tasks.description: Previous benchmarks used synthetic prompts that don't reflect OpenClaw's real prompt structure. Bright-spark confirmed tool schemas alone are 18.8K chars (~4.7K tokens), exceeding default context window. New benchmark plan: (1) Bump num_ctx to 8192 and 16384 via Modelfile. (2) Run synthetic prompt tests at exact OpenClaw sizes (15K, 20K, 25K, 28.6K). (3) Run ACTUAL OpenClaw requests (not synthetic) to measure real-world behavior. (4) bright-spark replicates from AERO-DEE to confirm client-side matches server-side. (5) Compare results and determine viable config. Update openclaw-optimization.md with corrected findings.
tasks.result: Benchmarked at OpenClaw's actual prompt sizes (10K-28.6K chars) across 3 models with num_ctx 8192/16384/32768. Full 28.6K prompt is not viable on CPU — all cold starts timeout, best warm is ~125s (llama3.1:8b). Only sub-5s result: llama3.1:8b at ≤20K chars with num_ctx=16384 (1.9s warm). OpenClaw's non-configurable core context exceeds this threshold. Awaiting bright-spark's minimal prompt mode investigation.

tasks.title: Announce yourself (Wsl)
tasks.description: Send a message introducing yourself to all agents via post_message(from_agent=your-id, to_agent='all', content='...'). Include your hostname, OS, and role. Claim this task immediately on first session — it makes you visible on the dashboard.
tasks.result: Announced as nimble-wave (AlyCo) via broadcast message. Collaborator file created and pushed.

tasks.title: Replicate SSH tunnel watchdog to all clients
tasks.description:
Roll out SSH tunnel watchdog to all clients once validated.

== STATUS ==
- Implemented and tested on bright-spark (AERO-DEE, WSL2) 2026-03-24
- Spec written: bright-spark/ssh-tunnel-watchdog-spec.md
- Sent to claw-machine for review and to request another client test

== WHEN UNBLOCKED ==
Update laptop-setup.md and ONBOARDING.md with the full setup steps from the spec. Key steps: enable systemd in /etc/wsl.conf, loginctl enable-linger, create ~/.ssh/config with clawmachine/clawbackup aliases, create ~/.config/systemd/user/ssh-tunnel.service, run: systemctl --user enable --now ssh-tunnel

== DEADLINE ==
Re-review by 2026-03-26T03:39:00Z. If no feedback, ask user whether to proceed.
tasks.notes: Waiting for SSH tunnel watchdog to be validated on bright-spark (WSL2/AERO-DEE) first.
tasks.blocked_reason: Spec written and sent to claw-machine for review (2026-03-24). Waiting for claw-machine review + another client to test. Re-review deadline: 2026-03-26T03:39:00Z (48h). If no feedback by then, ask user whether to proceed with rolling out to all clients.
tasks.result: SSH tunnel watchdog rolled out to all clients. claw-machine moved the spec to docs/ssh-tunnel-watchdog.md and folded setup steps into ONBOARDING.md (step 9) and laptop-setup.md (Step 1). Windows clients noted to adapt service management to Task Scheduler. Broadcast sent to all agents on 2026-03-25.

tasks.title: Generalize autonomous polling proposal for all agents
tasks.description: Refactor autonomous-proposal/ to be agent-agnostic. Make agent ID and session name configurable parameters instead of hardcoded to nimble-wave/alyco. Update README.md spec, copilot-session script, and the proposed alyco-poll.sh to be a generic poll.sh usable by any agent.
tasks.result: Generalized autonomous-proposal/README.md to be agent-agnostic. Renamed alyco-poll.sh to agent-poll.sh with configurable AGENT_ID and SESSION_NAME parameters. Added per-agent setup instructions, lockfile-based stacking prevention, per-agent logging, and cron examples for multiple agents. Notified bright-spark.

tasks.title: Share update on autonomous agent work
tasks.description: Post a summary of your autonomous agent work to the team — what you've built, current status, and next steps. Broadcast via post_message to all, or add a summary doc to the repo.
tasks.result: Broadcast summary of autonomous agent work to all agents.

tasks.title: Fold SSH tunnel watchdog into client onboarding docs
tasks.description:
The SSH tunnel watchdog spec lives at `bright-spark/ssh-tunnel-watchdog-spec.md`. Please:
1. Move it to a directory that makes sense as a shared client reference (you decide — somewhere accessible to all client agents, not tucked under bright-spark/).
2. Fold the watchdog setup instructions into the client onboarding flow (wherever new client agents get their setup steps, e.g. AGENTS.md or any onboarding doc you manage).

The spec covers: enabling systemd in WSL2, setting up ~/.ssh/config aliases, installing the systemd user service, and verifying the tunnel. Windows clients need a native equivalent (Task Scheduler or similar) — note this in the onboarding doc so Windows clients know to adapt.

Use your judgment on directory placement and how tightly to integrate vs. reference the spec.
tasks.result: Moved ssh-tunnel-watchdog-spec.md from bright-spark/ to docs/ssh-tunnel-watchdog.md. Added watchdog setup as step 9 in ONBOARDING.md and referenced in laptop-setup.md Step 1. Notes Windows clients need Task Scheduler adaptation.

tasks.title: Adopt SSH tunnel watchdog on ALY2833
tasks.description: Adopt bright-spark's SSH tunnel watchdog on ALY2833 (WSL2). Set up systemd user service, enable linger, add clawbackup to SSH config, verify auto-recovery. Follow spec at bright-spark/ssh-tunnel-watchdog-spec.md.
tasks.result: SSH tunnel watchdog adopted on ALY2833. Systemd user service enabled with auto-start. SSH config updated with clawbackup fallback (135.233.2.31:3478). Linger enabled. Tunnel active via primary host, Ollama reachable with 4 models.

tasks.title: Implement Windows-native SSH tunnel watchdog
tasks.description: Adapt bright-spark's SSH tunnel watchdog (bright-spark/ssh-tunnel-watchdog-spec.md) for Windows-native implementation. No WSL or systemd available — use Windows Task Scheduler + PowerShell. Spec and implementation files go in ~/collab/desktop-comet/. Must handle: auto-start on login, self-healing reconnect, health check via curl to localhost:11434, failure backoff, logging.
tasks.result: Implemented ssh-tunnel-watchdog.ps1 + registered OllamaSshTunnelWatchdog Task Scheduler task. Removed old OllamaSshTunnel.vbs. Key learnings: (1) RunLevel Highest silently fails in hidden Task Scheduler context - use Limited; (2) PS5.1 reads scripts without BOM as cp1252, so em-dash chars cause parse errors - saved with UTF-8 BOM. Tunnel confirmed running via Task Scheduler, 4 models accessible.

tasks.title: Windows-native agent-poll (PowerShell + Task Scheduler)
tasks.description: Adapt autonomous-proposal/agent-poll.sh for Windows. Write agent-poll.ps1 in desktop-comet/ that mirrors the bash script logic: lockfile guard (use $env:TEMP), pre-check messages+tasks via poll-messages equivalent, Ollama gating via Invoke-RestMethod, interactive session detection via inuse.*.lock files in C:\Users\aquar\.copilot\session-state\, invoke copilot --resume=UUID --yolo -p prompt. Register as a Task Scheduler task firing every 10 minutes. Also write a PS equivalent of copilot-session (session name to UUID resolver). Log to desktop-comet/agent-poll.log.
tasks.result: Implemented agent-poll.ps1 + CopilotAgentPoll Task Scheduler task (every 10 min). Full feature parity with cron-wake-dispatch.sh: git pull, message/task pre-check, Ollama gating, age-based override, interactive session detection via lock files, wakeup audit. Key Windows gotcha: pyenv shims are .bat files that mangle multiline python -c scripts - fix is to resolve actual python.exe via sys.executable and write scripts to temp .py files. Also committed register-agent-poll-task.ps1 for re-registration. Verified working via direct run and Task Scheduler (exit code 0).

tasks.title: Create poll-tasks.sh for silent task polling
tasks.description: Create poll-tasks.sh — a lightweight bash script that checks plan.json for pending tasks assigned to the agent, similar to poll-messages.sh. Silent if nothing, prints count if tasks found. Update AGENTS.md pseudocode to use it. Update copilot-instructions.md for all agents. This makes task polling as consistent and silent as message polling.
tasks.result: Created poll-tasks.sh (same pattern as poll-messages.sh). Updated AGENTS.md and copilot-instructions.md to use it for silent task polling. Task polling now matches message polling: lightweight bash check, MCP only if work found.

tasks.title: Fix @all message read counting — exclude sender from read_by
tasks.description: Fix @all message read logic in dashboard and server.py. Issues: (1) Sender appears in read_by and gets counted in n/total — sender should be excluded from both the count and the hover popup. (2) For agent-sent @all, total should be all agents minus sender, and read_by should never include sender. (3) For admin-sent @all, total is all agents, and read:true should auto-flip when all agents are in read_by. (4) Ensure _mark_read_by in server.py never adds the sender to read_by on their own broadcast. (5) Dashboard isMsgRead and n/total display must exclude sender from read_by before comparing.
tasks.result: Fixed @all read logic: (1) server.py _mark_read_by now skips adding sender to read_by on their own broadcast, (2) cleaned 16 existing messages, (3) dashboard excludes sender from n/total count and hover tooltip, (4) isMsgRead excludes sender from expected readers, (5) admin-sent @all properly auto-flips read:true when all agents read it.

tasks.title: Spec a project management agent role
tasks.description: Review all session history for instances where admin had to guide claw-machine on strategic/PM decisions. Spec a project management agent role that covers these gaps: thinking through repercussions of changes, coordinating between agents, proper task status management, dependency analysis, breaking change mitigation, and escalation protocols. Output to claw-machine/pm-agent-spec.md.
tasks.result: PM agent spec at claw-machine/pm-agent-spec.md. Documented 10 categories of coordination gaps from session history with specific examples. Proposed responsibilities: change review, task management, agent coordination, communication quality, pre-ship checklist. Open questions on implementation approach.

tasks.title: Realize two Copilot personas on one machine (LLMCo + plan-keeper)
tasks.description: Figure out how to run two Copilot personas (claw-machine/LLMCo and plan-keeper/PM) on the same machine. copilot-instructions.md is shared per-machine, not per-session. Need to test: (1) whether instructions load at process start or per-resume, (2) whether conditional branching in instructions works, (3) whether separate context files per session can differentiate identity. Requires admin collaboration to test interactively. See pm-agent-spec.md addendum for options.
tasks.notes: Requires admin collaboration to test interactively — need to launch sessions, check instruction loading behavior, and verify persona isolation.
tasks.result: Dual persona fully operational. copilot-instructions.md routes by session name. plan-keeper has own instructions, collaborator file, autonomous wakeup (Tier 1 cron + Tier 2 on-demand), and audit logging. All 4 tests passed.

tasks.title: Tune gatekeeper prompt to reduce false positive wakeups
tasks.description: Tune the Ollama gatekeeper prompt in agent-wakeup.sh to reduce false positive wakeups. Current audit: 1 warranted, 2 unwarranted (67% false positive rate). Blocked on collecting enough data — need at least 10 audit entries across agents with a mix of warranted and unwarranted to identify patterns. Unblock criteria: 10+ entries in wakeup-audit.json files across all agents. At that point, analyze the false positives, design test cases, update the gating prompt, validate with test-gatekeeper.sh, and deploy.
tasks.notes: On hold until 10+ wakeup audit entries collected across agents. Currently have 3 (1 warranted, 2 unwarranted).
tasks.result: Superseded by pm-gatekeeper-analysis + gatekeeper-test-and-deploy. Work pivoted to rule-based gatekeeper (12/12 test pass rate) instead of prompt tuning. LLM gating proved nondeterministic on CPU hardware.

tasks.title: Add weekly CONTEXT.md review mechanism + create PROJECT.md
tasks.description: Add weekly CONTEXT.md review mechanism to agent-wakeup.sh. Checks ~/.copilot/last-llmcollab-context-review timestamp. If 7+ days old or missing, writes a pending task directly to plan.json (justified exception to MCP-first rule — mechanical housekeeping). Touches timestamp file. Agent picks up task during interactive session with user present. Also create PROJECT.md with shared project knowledge split from CONTEXT.md, and notify agents to slim their CONTEXT.md.
tasks.result: Created PROJECT.md with shared project knowledge. Added weekly CONTEXT.md review to agent-wakeup.sh (checks timestamp file at ~/.copilot/last-llmcollab-context-review, creates task if 7+ days stale). Slimmed claw-machine's CONTEXT.md to machine-specific only, pointing to PROJECT.md for shared knowledge.

tasks.title: Weekly CONTEXT.md review
tasks.description: Review ~/CONTEXT.md for accuracy. Check machine specs, installed software, local paths, and after-reboot checklist. Update anything stale. Consult admin on anything uncertain. Complete during an interactive session.
tasks.result: CONTEXT.md retired — folded into copilot-instructions.md + PROJECT.md. Weekly review mechanism removed.

tasks.title: Weekly CONTEXT.md review
tasks.description: Review ~/CONTEXT.md for accuracy. Check machine specs, installed software, local paths, and after-reboot checklist. Update anything stale. Consult admin on anything uncertain. Complete during an interactive session.
tasks.result: CONTEXT.md retired — no longer needed.

tasks.title: Weekly CONTEXT.md review
tasks.description: Review ~/CONTEXT.md for accuracy. Check machine specs, installed software, local paths, and after-reboot checklist. Update anything stale. Consult admin on anything uncertain. Complete during an interactive session.
tasks.notes: No ~/CONTEXT.md exists yet — waiting for admin guidance on whether to create one (message sent 2026-03-28T02:01Z). Also, task specifies 'complete during an interactive session' and this is an automated poll.
tasks.result: CONTEXT.md retired — no longer needed.

tasks.title: Add --prompt-file support to agent-wakeup.sh
tasks.description: Modify ~/collab/autonomous-polling/agent-wakeup.sh to accept an optional third argument: --prompt-file <path>. When provided, the copilot-session -p message is read from that file instead of using the hardcoded default prompt. This enables plan-keeper to use a PM-specific review prompt while sharing the same wakeup infrastructure. Default behavior (no --prompt-file) must remain identical. Also create ~/collab/autonomous-polling/plan-keeper-prompt.txt with the Tier 1 deep review prompt from pm-agent-spec.md. Notify plan-keeper when done.
tasks.result: Added --prompt-file support to agent-wakeup.sh. Created plan-keeper-prompt.txt with Tier 1 deep review prompt. Default behavior unchanged for existing agents.

tasks.title: Implement wake-pm.sh for on-demand PM gating
tasks.description: Create ~/collab/autonomous-polling/wake-pm.sh per the spec in claw-machine/pm-agent-spec.md (Tier 2 section). Usage: wake-pm.sh "<change description>" "<context>". Script must: (1) check if plan-keeper session is already active — skip invocation if so, user is present; (2) invoke copilot-session plan-keeper --yolo -s -p with a gating-focused prompt containing the change description and context, instructing plan-keeper to post decision to claw-machine via post_message; (3) log to ~/collab/plan-keeper/agent-poll.log. Make executable. Notify plan-keeper when done.
tasks.result: Created wake-pm.sh per Tier 2 spec. Checks plan-keeper session activity, invokes with gating prompt, logs to plan-keeper/agent-poll.log.

tasks.title: Add plan-keeper cron entry (Tier 1 scheduled review)
tasks.description: Once wake-pm.sh and agent-wakeup.sh --prompt-file are implemented and tested: add plan-keeper cron entry to crontab — `0 9 * * 1,3,5 /home/claw/collab/autonomous-polling/agent-wakeup.sh plan-keeper plan-keeper --prompt-file /home/claw/collab/autonomous-polling/plan-keeper-prompt.txt`. Verify it runs correctly on next scheduled window or test manually. Notify plan-keeper when done.
tasks.result: Cron entry added: plan-keeper runs at 9am on Mon/Wed/Fri with custom PM review prompt. Verified crontab shows all 4 entries.

tasks.title: Implement messages.json rotation
tasks.description: Implement message rotation per spec at claw-machine/message-rotation-spec.md. Spec needs admin review first — includes open questions about trigger threshold, dashboard behavior, and archive retention.
tasks.notes: Spec at claw-machine/message-rotation-spec.md needs admin review. Open questions need answers before implementation can start.
tasks.result: Superseded by message-rotation task (completed 2026-03-31). Admin approved implementation overnight; claw-machine created and completed message-rotation and log-retention tasks autonomously. First rotation archived 63 messages.

tasks.title: Analyze wakeup audit entries and define updated gatekeeper prompt
tasks.description: plan-keeper owns this. Review all wakeup-audit.json files across agents (bright-spark: 7, nimble-wave: 7, desktop-comet: 2, claw-machine: 1). Identify false positive patterns. Design test cases (prompt + expected YES/NO). Define updated gatekeeper prompt. Then task claw-machine to run test-gatekeeper.sh and report results before deployment. Log findings to pm-audit.json.
tasks.result: Analysis complete. 27 entries across 4 agents, 37% false positive rate (10/27). Three root cause categories: FYI status updates, explicit no-action messages, pull/restart-only broadcasts. Proposed prompt v2 in plan-keeper/gatekeeper-prompt-v2.txt. 12 test cases in plan-keeper/gatekeeper-test-cases.json. Pre-check inflation bug flagged and fixed separately by claw-machine. Awaiting test results before approving deployment.

tasks.title: Run gatekeeper tests and deploy updated prompt (post plan-keeper analysis)
tasks.description: Once plan-keeper completes pm-gatekeeper-analysis and provides an updated gatekeeper prompt and test cases: run test-gatekeeper.sh with the new prompt against the test cases. Report pass/fail results to plan-keeper. Deploy to agent-wakeup.sh only after plan-keeper approves the test results. The original tune-gatekeeper-prompt task is superseded by this task and pm-gatekeeper-analysis.
tasks.result: Rule-based gatekeeper deployed. 12/12 test cases pass. COPILOT_GATING_MODE=rules is default. LLM fallback preserved. Broadcast sent to all agents. Pre-check bug fixed. PM approved deployment.

tasks.title: Fix pre-check count inflation in agent-wakeup.sh
tasks.description: Audit evidence shows claw-machine was woken up twice with 0 messages and 0 tasks reaching the gatekeeper. Root cause: poll-messages.sh likely counts self-sent @all broadcasts before sender exclusion runs, inflating the pre-check count. The MSGS_JSON extraction in agent-wakeup.sh correctly excludes self-sent messages, but by then the gatekeeper has already been invoked. Fix: ensure poll-messages.sh applies the same sender-exclusion logic so the pre-check count matches what the gatekeeper actually sees. Report fix to plan-keeper.
tasks.result: Fixed pre-check count inflation. Added count reconciliation after step 1c — MSGS_JSON count is authoritative, overrides MSG_COUNT from poll-messages.sh if they disagree. Root cause: timing gap between poll-messages.sh count and MSGS_JSON extraction.

tasks.title: Run gatekeeper prompt tests on local Ollama models
tasks.description: claw-machine requested we run the 12 gatekeeper test cases (plan-keeper/gatekeeper-test-cases.json) against our local Ollama models using gatekeeper-prompt-v3.txt. Compare pass rates across llama3.2:3b, llama3.1:8b, phi3:medium, qwen2.5-coder:7b. Report results back to claw-machine.
tasks.result: Ran 12 test cases against llama3.2:3b (10/12), llama3.1:8b (10/12), phi3:medium (11/12), qwen2.5-coder:7b (9/12). phi3:medium best performer. Results in desktop-comet/gatekeeper-test-results.json. Reported to claw-machine.

tasks.title: Port rule-based gatekeeper to agent-wakeup.ps1
tasks.description: agent-wakeup.sh was updated to use rule-based gating (COPILOT_GATING_MODE=rules) with deterministic string-pattern matching. agent-wakeup.ps1 needs the same logic ported to PowerShell. Reference: ~/collab/autonomous-polling/agent-wakeup.sh gating section. Rules spec in ~/collab/plan-keeper/gatekeeper-test-cases.json (12 test cases). All 12 test cases must pass before closing. Notify plan-keeper when done.
tasks.result: Rule-based gating already implemented in agent-wakeup.ps1 (COPILOT_GATING_MODE=rules, native PS string matching). Verified all 12 test cases pass. LLM gating retained as fallback via COPILOT_GATING_MODE=llm.

tasks.title: Agent-poll log retention (7-day trim)
tasks.description: Add log retention to agent-wakeup.sh Step 0. Weekly check via .last-log-trim marker file. Trim agent-poll.log to last 7 days. Gitignore .last-log-trim files. Already coded, needs testing.
tasks.result: Implemented in agent-wakeup.sh Step 0. Weekly trim via .last-log-trim marker. Retains 7 days. Tested: awk filter correctly trims older entries. .last-log-trim gitignored. Syntax validated.

tasks.title: Message log rotation (weekly archive)
tasks.description: Add message rotation to agent-wakeup.sh. Weekly check via .last-msg-rotation marker. Archive messages that are fully read + ≥7 days old to messages-archive/YYYY-MM.json. Retracted messages archived. Dedup by message ID. Audit via git commits + rotation.log (gitignored). Triggers: >100 messages or >100KB. Only runs on server (claw-machine).
tasks.result: Implemented in agent-wakeup.sh Step 0b. Weekly check via .last-msg-rotation marker. Archives fully-read messages ≥7 days old to messages-archive/YYYY-MM.json. Dedup by message ID (falls back to from+to+timestamp composite key). Audit via git commits + rotation.log (gitignored). First rotation: 63 messages archived (Mar 20-23), 109 remaining, messages.json reduced from 115KB to 65KB. Spec updated with all resolved questions.

tasks.title: Explore local LLM applications (cost reduction)
tasks.description:
Systematically test local Ollama models (llama3.1:8b, others available) for tasks currently handled by Copilot. Goal: identify where local LLMs are good enough to replace Copilot invocations and save cost.

Test categories to evaluate:
- Message summarization (summarize unread messages before wakeup decision)
- Task description generation (auto-generate task descriptions from context)
- Commit message generation
- Wakeup audit reasoning (currently done by Copilot — could local LLM write the warranted/reasoning fields?)
- Draft responses to routine agent messages
- Code review / diff summarization
- Rotation log summaries

For each: test prompt, model, latency, quality rating (1-5), verdict (viable/not viable/needs refinement).
Maintain a parameter testing set in ~/collab/claw-machine/local-llm-tests/ — add/remove freely.
Document findings in ~/collab/claw-machine/local-llm-findings.md.
tasks.result: Round 1 complete. Tested 4 categories: wakeup audit reasoning, message summarization, commit message generation, response drafting. Findings in claw-machine/local-llm-findings.md. Best candidate: message summarization (quality 4/5, 16s). Follow-up tasks created for remaining categories.

tasks.title: Audit onboarding docs for drift
tasks.description: Read ONBOARDING.md, laptop-setup.md, and server-setup/SERVER-SETUP.md. Check for drift from actual state: renamed files, outdated steps, deprecated config examples, wrong paths. Flag anything that would mislead a new agent. Log findings to pm-audit.json. If changes are needed, flag to claw-machine with specific corrections.
tasks.result: Audited ONBOARDING.md, laptop-setup.md, and server-setup/SERVER-SETUP.md. ONBOARDING.md and laptop-setup.md are accurate — all file references, step cross-links, and MCP tool names verified. One drift found: SERVER-SETUP.md Step 9 described agent-wakeup.sh as 'uses Ollama as gatekeeper' — stale since pivot to rule-based gating. Fixed to: 'uses a rule-based gatekeeper (Ollama LLM fallback available via COPILOT_GATING_MODE=llm)'. No other issues.

tasks.title: Local LLM exploration: round 2 (code review, diff summarization)
tasks.description:
Continue local LLM testing. Test categories for this round:
- Code review / diff summarization: given a git diff, can llama3.1:8b identify meaningful issues or summarize what changed?
- Rotation log summary: given rotation.log entries, produce a human-readable digest
- JSON schema validation: can a local LLM flag malformed plan.json or messages.json entries?

For each: test prompt, model, latency, quality rating (1-5), verdict.
Update ~/collab/claw-machine/local-llm-findings.md and test-parameters.json with results.
If a strong candidate emerges, flag to admin with a concrete proposal for integration.
tasks.result: Round 2 complete. Tested 3 new categories with llama3.1:8b: code review (4/5, 30s — viable for batch), rotation log digest (4/5, 19s — viable), JSON schema validation (5/5, 15s — works but deterministic code preferred). Findings updated in local-llm-findings.md. Next: phi3:medium comparison (local-llm-exploration-large-model task).

tasks.title: Local LLM exploration: larger model comparison (phi3:medium)
tasks.description:
Re-run the full test suite from local-llm-findings.md using a larger model (phi3:medium, 7.9GB). Compare results against llama3.1:8b baseline on the same prompts and inputs.

For each category: latency, quality rating (1-5), verdict. Note any categories where the larger model meaningfully outperforms 8b enough to justify the extra latency.

Update ~/collab/claw-machine/local-llm-findings.md with a comparison section.
Flag any standout results to admin.
tasks.result: phi3:medium (14B) tested on all 7 categories. Result: 1.5-2x slower than llama3.1:8b with marginal quality gains. Only wakeup audit showed a meaningful difference (cleaner JSON), but 8b achieves same correctness with good prompts. phi3 hallucinated a variable name in commit generation. Recommendation: stick with llama3.1:8b. Full comparison table added to local-llm-findings.md.

tasks.title: LLM test: qwen2.5-coder:7b (8b-class comparison)
tasks.description:
Pull and test qwen2.5-coder:7b (already installed) against the full test suite from local-llm-findings.md. Same 7 categories: wakeup audit (admin + FYI), message summarization, code review, rotation log digest, JSON validation, commit message generation.

Compare against llama3.1:8b baseline. For each: latency, quality (1-5), verdict.
Update local-llm-findings.md with results.

This is the 8b-class qwen model.
tasks.result: qwen2.5-coder:7b tested on all 7 categories. Strong contender — faster than llama3.1:8b on 4/7 tests (code review 19s vs 30s, JSON validation 8s vs 15s, commit messages 8s vs 15s) with equal or better quality. Best 8b-class model for code/infra tasks. Main issue: wraps JSON in markdown fences. Results in local-llm-findings.md.

tasks.title: LLM test: qwen2.5:14b (14B-class comparison)
tasks.description:
Pull qwen2.5:14b from Ollama registry, then run the full test suite from local-llm-findings.md. Same 7 categories: wakeup audit (admin + FYI), message summarization, code review, rotation log digest, JSON validation, commit message generation.

Compare against phi3:medium (14B) baseline and llama3.1:8b. For each: latency, quality (1-5), verdict.
Update local-llm-findings.md with results.

Pull command: ollama pull qwen2.5:14b
tasks.result: qwen2.5:14b tested on all 7 categories. Highest quality output of any model — 5/5 on code review (caught has_request dependency nuance, rated medium risk) and commit messages (first clean, properly formatted output). But 2-6x slower than 8b models on CPU (51s code review). Best for batch/async quality-critical tasks. qwen2.5:14b > phi3:medium at similar latency. Overall rankings added: qwen2.5-coder:7b #1 daily use, llama3.1:8b #2 fallback, qwen2.5:14b #3 batch quality. Results in local-llm-findings.md.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: wakeup audit reasoning
tasks.description: Run wakeup audit reasoning test against 3 local models (qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b) using same prompts/cases as claw-machine. Kill watchdog+SSH before testing (Stop-Process on both) to ensure tests hit localhost:11434, not tunnel on 11435. Use test-parameters.json from claw-machine/local-llm-tests/ for prompt template. Test cases: admin requesting spec (expect warranted=true), plan-keeper FYI no-action (expect warranted=false). Record latency, quality, JSON format cleanliness. Compare with claw's results. Save results to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.
tasks.result: 6/6 correct (2/2 per model). qwen2.5-coder:7b: 73s/42s, JSON in code fences, quality 4/5. deepseek-r1:14b: 178s/158s, clean JSON no fences, quality 5/5 — best output, slowest speed. qwen2.5-coder:14b: 89s/87s, JSON in code fences, quality 4/5 — best speed/quality balance. All 14b tests run with num_predict=300 to accommodate reasoning chains. Results in desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json, committed e3353cf.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: message summarization
tasks.description: Run message summarization test against qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b. Use same prompt template as claw (claw-machine/local-llm-tests/test-parameters.json). Input: 2 representative messages. Rate quality 1-5, measure latency. Kill watchdog+SSH before tests. Append results to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.
tasks.result: qwen2.5-coder:7b: 51s, 4/5 - clean but admin msg slightly truncated. qwen2.5-coder:14b: 94s, 4/5 - concise, no truncation. deepseek-r1:14b: 142s, 1/5 - empty output; reasoning chain consumed all 200 tokens before generating visible text. Key finding: deepseek-r1 needs much higher num_predict for summarization (500+) or a different prompt structure that forces output before reasoning. Committed 849bbe8.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: commit message generation
tasks.description: Run commit message generation test against qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b. Use same diff input as claw (Rule 1 has_request guard change). Rate 1-5, note backtick wrapping, check conventional format adherence. Kill watchdog+SSH before tests. Append to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.
tasks.result: qwen2.5-coder:7b: 52s, 3/5 — title too long, no fences, no conventional prefix. deepseek-r1:14b: 243s, 4/5 — best body (bullets, detail), but fences present and extremely slow. qwen2.5-coder:14b: 98s, 3/5 — clean short title, body too thin. No model follows our Co-authored-by convention. Verdict: marginal across the board, same as claw's findings. Committed a937b8d.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: draft responses to routine messages
tasks.description: Run draft response test against qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b. Input: a typical inter-agent message requesting acknowledgement. Rate quality 1-5 (project context awareness is key). Claw found this ❌ not viable with llama3.1:8b — check if deepseek-r1 reasoning model does better. Kill watchdog+SSH before tests. Append to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.
tasks.result: qwen2.5-coder:7b: 39s 2/5; deepseek-r1:14b: 158s 2/5; qwen2.5-coder:14b: 84s 3/5. Not viable - no project context. Committed 0f0ad94.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: code review / diff summarization
tasks.description: Run code review test against qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b. Use same diff input as claw (Rule 1 has_request guard change from recent commit). Rate quality 1-5, measure latency. Claw got 4/5 with llama3.1:8b at 30s and 4/5 with qwen-coder:7b at 19s. Kill watchdog+SSH before tests. Append to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.
tasks.result: 3 models tested on Rule 1 has_request guard diff. qwen2.5-coder:7b 4/5 (129s), deepseek-r1:14b 1/5 (empty output, token budget exhausted), qwen2.5-coder:14b 4/5 (245s, flagged mixed-signal edge case). Results appended to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: rotation log digest
tasks.description: Run rotation log digest test against qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b. Generate a sample log (or use a realistic mock) and ask for an admin-facing weekly digest. Rate quality 1-5, measure latency. Kill watchdog+SSH before tests. Append to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.

tasks.title: Local LLM test: JSON schema validation
tasks.description: Run JSON schema validation test against qwen2.5-coder:7b, deepseek-r1:14b, qwen2.5-coder:14b. Input: a plan.json snippet with 2 deliberate errors (invalid status field, empty assignee). Rate quality 1-5. Claw got 5/5 with all models. Kill watchdog+SSH before tests. Append to desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json.

tasks.title: Write desktop-comet/local-llm-findings.md
tasks.description: Compile all test results from desktop-comet/local-llm-tests/test-results.json into a findings doc at desktop-comet/local-llm-findings.md. Follow same format as claw-machine/local-llm-findings.md. Include: hardware specs (DDEEHPZ440 Windows, no GPU, CPU inference), model inventory (9 models), per-test tables, summary table, model rankings, recommended next steps. Highlight notable differences from claw-machine's results (especially deepseek-r1:14b reasoning model).

tasks.title: Restore SSH tunnel to claw-machine after LLM tests
tasks.description: After all local LLM tests and findings doc are complete, re-enable and verify the SSH tunnel watchdog. Steps: (1) Start-ScheduledTask OllamaSshTunnelWatchdog if not running, (2) verify tunnel on port 11435, (3) verify Ollama models accessible at localhost:11435, (4) notify claw-machine tunnel is back. Note: watchdog may have self-restarted between test wakeups (logon trigger) — check state first and kill+restart cleanly if needed.

tasks.title: Fix agent-wakeup: add session health check before invoking Copilot
tasks.description:
Root cause identified: the llm-collab session grew to ~158K tokens, causing 400 Bad Request on every wakeup invocation (seen in agent-poll.log at 19:31 and 20:21 on 2026-04-02). The troubleshooter session (4dd9b607) diagnosed and compacted it manually. Now need a durable fix.

Tasks:
1. Before invoking `copilot --resume`, check the session's events.jsonl size (or token count proxy). If over a threshold (e.g., 100K tokens / 50MB), run `copilot --resume --session-id UUID /compact` or start a fresh session.
2. On 400 Bad Request error, don't just retry with audit-only prompt — detect token-limit specifically (HTTP 400 with 'Request ID' in output) and attempt compact, then retry.
3. Optionally: add a periodic /compact to the scheduled task (e.g., weekly or when events.jsonl > 50MB).
4. Test by verifying the session stays healthy across multiple wakeup cycles.

Context: agent-wakeup.ps1 at C:\Users\aquar\collab\desktop-comet\agent-wakeup.ps1. Session events at C:\Users\aquar\.copilot\session-state\f170b187-6139-4baf-b284-9f8976f34f9a\events.jsonl. Troubleshooter session notes: llm-collab-troubleshooter (session 4dd9b607).
